title: Add machine-readable observability contract endpoint for TPS snapshots, API responses, and Prometheus metrics metadata

requirements.text: Expose a stable machine-readable observability contract with contract version and plugin version.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: A JSON-compatible contract is available via a documented API route and/or helper and includes contract version plus plugin version.
Contract lists snapshot fields, scalar types, units, freshness semantics, and session mismatch guidance.

requirements.text: Define API and WebSocket payload metadata.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Contract identifies existing TPS REST/WebSocket surfaces and describes response/event payload fields without altering those responses.
Contract lists metric names, types, units, and label cardinality expectations.
Existing plugin registration, hook path, status snapshot injection, REST routes, WebSocket behavior, and metrics output remain additive-only.
Contract generation is static or cheap and does not inspect all sessions or query large SQLite tables.
README explains endpoint/helper location, contract versioning, and how consumers should validate or ignore unknown fields.
Tests assert required contract sections and representative fields/metrics, including optional dependency behavior.

success_criteria: A machine-readable observability contract is exposed at a stable documented location.
The contract includes field names, types, units, freshness semantics, metric types, and label/cardinality notes for current surfaces.
Existing behavior is additive-only and unchanged.
README documents contract versioning and consumer tolerance for unknown fields.

graph_fit.track: track-A adjunct after current in-progress API/performance work
graph_fit.dedup: No open or in-progress br search match; semantic search returned adjacent closed/API/Prometheus/dashboard work but no contract endpoint bead.
